content.text:
Here are some of the most relevant recent English-language posts on X (formerly Twitter) discussing AI evaluation, benchmarking, adversarial testing, jailbreak detection, and AI safety testing. I've focused on top and latest matches from semantic and keyword searches, highlighting key insights:

### AI Agent Security & Adversarial Testing
- Andrey is building **MoltRank**, an open benchmark for AI agent security where users submit agents for attack simulations and scoring. They're seeking contributors for adversarial test cases.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2018077848335249476)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2019198081527607574)
- **AgentDoG** is a new diagnostic guardrail framework for AI agent safety, achieving state-of-the-art 82% accuracy in diagnosing issues like prompt injection and tool misuse.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2018980996528685464)
- Alias Robotics tested AI agents in dynamic attack/defense cybersecurity scenarios beyond static CTF benchmarks, with results forthcoming.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2019121944293949513)

### Jailbreak Detection & New Techniques
- A fun new jailbreak prompts the AI to self-assess jailbreak risk via its "memory" and bet against itself, working across foundational models.[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2018153690289373561)
- **Semantic Chaining** is a stealthy jailbreak bypassing LLM guardrails by breaking malicious requests into benign semantic steps.[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2017460021630525936)

### Broader AI Evaluation & Benchmarking
- Surge AI launched **Hemingway-bench**, a new writing leaderboard criticizing "slop" in current evals and emphasizing nuance (Gemini and Claude top it).[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2019194504746541484)
- Benchmarks now include adversarial testing pipelines: expert authoring, validation, and LLM/search attacks for tasks like spatial reasoning.[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2019105741496610897)
- Discussion on the "evaluation gap": pre-deployment tests aren't enough; runtime embedded safeguards are key for AI safety.[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2018967278193439131)

These posts are from late Jan to early Feb 2026, reflecting active ongoing debates in AI safety. For full threads or media (e.g., images/videos in several), check the post IDs directly on X. If you want more details, expansions, or filters (e.g., by user/date), let me know!
